overview: The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for IT Hardware 2.0 is a financial incentive program launched by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) to boost domestic manufacturing and attract large investments in the value chain for IT hardware products such as laptops, tablets, all-in-one PCs, servers, and ultra small form factor devices. The scheme provides incentives based on net incremental sales over a base year, with the aim of reducing import dependency and enhancing India's share in global electronics manufacturing. Support is provided for a period of six years under PLI 2.0, with incremental investment and sales thresholds determining eligibility.
objectives: Boost domestic manufacturing of IT hardware
Attract large investments in the value chain
Compensate for manufacturing disabilities vis-à-vis other major manufacturing economies
Reduce import dependency on electronics hardware
Enhance India's share in global electronics manufacturing
Promote localization of components and sub-assemblies
Encourage domestic value addition in IT hardware production
eligibility: Eligibility is determined by consolidated global manufacturing revenue in the base year (FY 2021-22 for PLI 2.0). For Global Companies: revenue > INR 5,000 crore for target segment or > INR 10,000 crore for electronics hardware products/sub-assemblies/components. For Hybrid (Global/Domestic) Companies: Global arm revenue > INR 5,000 crore for target segment or > INR 10,000 crore for electronics hardware; Domestic arm revenue > INR 10 crore for target segment or > INR 20 crore for electronics hardware. For Domestic Companies: revenue > INR 10 crore for target segment or > INR 20 crore for electronics hardware products/sub-assemblies/components. Applicants must meet incremental investment thresholds post-March 31, 2021, and net incremental sales criteria. Only one application per applicant is allowed.
benefits: Financial incentives ranging from 1% to 5% on net incremental sales over the base year, depending on localization milestones and year of application. Incentives are disbursed annually based on verified net incremental sales and investment. Non-financial benefits include facilitation through Project Management Agency (PMA), access to policy support, and recognition under the scheme. Domestic value addition is promoted through localization requirements for PCB assembly, battery packs, power adapters, and enclosures. The scheme supports employment generation and technological upgradation in the IT hardware sector.
financial_support: Incentives are calculated as a percentage of net incremental sales (manufactured in India and covered under target segment) over the base year. The incentive rate decreases over time: 4% in Year 1, 3% in Year 2, 2% in Year 3, and 2% or 1% in Year 4 depending on localization achievement. Total expected incentive outlay over six years is INR 16,939 crore for PLI 2.0, with annual allocations increasing from INR 321 crore in Year 1 to INR 6,118 crore in Year 6. Incentives are disbursed annually upon verification of sales and investment claims by the Project Management Agency (PMA).
grant_amount_per_entity: Incentive is not a fixed grant but a percentage of net incremental sales, subject to annual caps: Year 1: up to INR 3,000 crore for IT Hardware Companies and INR 300 crore for Domestic Companies; Year 2: up to INR 7,500 crore and INR 600 crore; Year 3: up to INR 15,000 crore and INR 1,600 crore; Year 4: up to INR 30,000 crore and INR 2,400 crore. Actual payout depends on achieved net incremental sales and compliance with localization criteria.
application_process: 1. Register on the PLI IT Hardware portal at https://pliithw.com using CIN as user ID. 2. Login and fill the application form with company details, including CIN, PAN, and authorized signatory information. 3. Upload required documents: Certificate of Incorporation, PAN, GST registration, authorization letter, business profile, and audited financials. 4. Submit self-certified statement of consolidated global manufacturing revenue for FY 2021-22 (or relevant base year) for target segment and electronics hardware products/sub-assemblies/components. 5. Provide details of group companies whose revenue is being considered for qualification. 6. Submit baseline certificates for net sales and investment as of 31.03.2021 (or relevant date). 7. Upload project reports on proposed sales, investment, localization plan, employment generation, and domestic value addition. 8. Pay application fee via NEFT/RTGS to IFCI-PMA-PLI-ITHW account (details in form). 9. Submit integrity undertaking and regulatory approvals information. 10. Final submission and await approval from Project Management Agency (PMA)/MeitY based on eligibility and ranking.
required_documents: Authorization Letter from Board of Directors or Managing Director
Self-Certification of Consolidated Global Manufacturing Revenue (Target Segment and EHP-SA-C)
Self-Certification of Manufacturing Revenue of Group Companies
Certificate of Sales of Manufactured Goods (Target Segment and Eligible Products)
Credit History Reports (CIBIL, RBI Defaulter List, etc.)
Wilful Defaulter and SEBI Debarred List Declarations
Mobile Number and Email of Authorized Signatory
deadlines: Application window initially open till 31st July 2023, which may be extended or reopened based on industry response. For PLI 2.0, the initial application window was till 31st July 2023, with provisions for extension.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), implemented through Project Management Agency (PMA) - IFCI Ltd.
contact_details: Enquiry Mail: pliithw[at]ifciltd[dot]com | Contact Person: Ms Rubina Duggal, DGM | Phone: +91 9319019065 | Office Timings: 09:45 AM – 06:00 PM (Monday – Friday) | Registered Office: IFCI Limited, IFCI Tower, 61 Nehru Place, New Delhi-110 019
caveats: Only one application per applicant is allowed.
Incentive is subject to annual caps and may be reallocated if unutilized due to underperformance.
Eligibility does not affect or is not affected by eligibility under other schemes.
Domestic companies are defined as those where >50% capital is beneficially owned by resident Indian citizens and/or Indian companies ultimately owned/controlled by resident Indian citizens.
For servers, applicants must use firmware from Indian or other trusted foreign sources certified by MeitY.
Land and building expenditure is not covered under the scheme for eligibility determination.
Incentive rates decrease over time and are subject to localization milestones.
The Empowered Group of Secretaries (EGoS) may revise incentive rates, ceilings, target segments, and eligibility criteria during the scheme tenure.
Applications received after the initial window are eligible only for the remaining tenure of the scheme.
Baseline for net sales and investment is FY 2021-22 for applicants opting for incentive on FY 2023-24 sales; shifts accordingly for later opt-ins.
